---
title: "Structural Bioinformatics: Exercise 2"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
authors: "Marta Aransay, Carlota de la Concepción, Marta Rodrigo, Lucía Santos"
date: 10/03/2026
bibliography: bibliografia.bib
csl: vancouver.csl  
---

# Goal of the project

This is a CASP-like exercise. The objective of this contest is to achieve the best possible predicted structure for at least three sequences using state-of-the-art protein modeling methodologies. For each sequence, you must utilize three or more different protocols for model generation and select the most optimal model.

# Background information of the selected proteins

{{< include docs/Protein1_bg.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein2_bg.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein3_bg.qmd >}}

# Protein modeling

## Protein 1: ORAI1

{{< include docs/Protein1_1Dfeatures.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein1_complete.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein1_evaluation.qmd >}}

## Protein 2

{{< include docs/Protein2_1Dfeatures.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein2_complete.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein2_evaluation.qmd >}}

## Protein 3: Osm1

{{< include docs/Protein3_1Dfeatures.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein3_complete.qmd >}}
{{< include docs/Protein3_evaluation.qmd >}}

# Results

# References
